laws.title: Public Law 15-101 | Ensuring Accountability in Federal Agencies Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source text is the Guidance to Guidances Act: it creates agency guidance-publication rules, a FOIA carve-out, and related reporting mechanics. No repeal or sunset appears in the source or later-law materials reviewed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title for the Guidance to Guidances Act.
provisions.exact_change: Keep a concise title 5 statutory note.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the Guidance to Guidances Act."
provisions.notes: The manifest heading is a mislabel.

provisions.text_summary: Defines agency and guidance document by reference to title 5.
provisions.exact_change: Record the title 5 reference definitions as note material.
provisions.evidence: "agency has the meaning given that term in section 551 of title 5"

provisions.text_summary: Requires publication of guidance documents and a designated board or posting mechanism.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the guidance-publication mandate as a concise note.
provisions.evidence: "the agency shall publish the guidance document"
provisions.notes: Includes the Trello-board style archive mechanism from the source text.

provisions.text_summary: Exempts FOIA-exempt material from the publication duty.
provisions.exact_change: Record the FOIA carve-out only.
provisions.evidence: "exempt from disclosure under section 552(b) of title 5"

provisions.text_summary: Severability and immediate effectiveness.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the closing clauses as standard note material.
provisions.evidence: "If any provision of this Act"; "This bill will take effect immediately"

current_implementation.detail: The title 5 anchor is the right subject-matter home, but the current note should be shortened and de-linked. The source is current; the problem is codification hygiene, not legal validity.

recommended_actions: Retain a concise statutory note at the title 5 anchor.
Remove the archive/Trello link.
Do not treat the guidance-publication text as a permanent positive-law rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 15-102 | New Ambassadorial Organizational and Reporting Act
laws.status_basis: The source remains operative. It expressly repeals the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act, repeals PL 14-90 section 2(a)(i), creates regional ambassadorships, and imposes weekly reporting duties.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, anti-supersession, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as note material.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the New Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act in full.
provisions.exact_change: Repeat-mark the earlier ambassadorial act as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act shall be repealed in its entirety"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals PL 14-90 section 2(a)(i).
provisions.exact_change: Strike the cited acting-waiver clause.
provisions.evidence: "subsection (i) ... shall be repealed in its entirety"

provisions.text_summary: Creates regional ambassadorships and assigns their jurisdiction.
provisions.exact_change: Record the regional ambassadorship framework as note text.
provisions.evidence: "The following regional ambassadorships shall be created under this Act"

provisions.text_summary: Requires weekly reports to the President, Secretary of State, and foreign-affairs committees.
provisions.exact_change: Record the weekly reporting duty as note text.
provisions.evidence: "shall submit ... weekly reports of their work"

current_implementation.detail: A boilerplate statutory note exists, but it does not implement the express repeals or any later amendment cross-reference. The entry should be an honest amendment note, not a generic history stub.

recommended_actions: Implement the repeal of the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act and PL 14-90 section 2(a)(i).
Add the regional-ambassadorship and weekly-reporting provisions as concise note material.
Remove the archive/Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-104 | The Military Justice Reorganization and Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Public Law 29-210 expressly repeals PL 15-104 and PL 19-146 and states that the repeals do not revive earlier Code text. The later-law decision leaves no present XML operation safely determinable.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and armed-services definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Retain as repeal history only.
provisions.evidence: "The Military Justice Reorganization and Reform Act"; "This Act may be cited as"

provisions.text_summary: Chief Judge Advocate General office, responsibilities, reform measures, reporting, and oversight.
provisions.exact_change: Do not preserve as operative Code text.
provisions.evidence: "The Chief Judge Advocate General shall be the Chief Legal Officer"

provisions.text_summary: Branch Judge Advocates General and Deputy JAGs.
provisions.exact_change: Retain only as repealed-history material.
provisions.evidence: "Each United States Armed Services Branch shall be granted a Judge Advocate General"

provisions.text_summary: Military courts, military judges, and the ethics review board.
provisions.exact_change: Do not create a live Title 10 section from this act.
provisions.evidence: "Each branch of the Armed Services shall establish military courts"

provisions.text_summary: Confidentiality, legal personnel, and military paralegals.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only as repealed-history material.
provisions.evidence: "The attorney-client privilege shall be recognized and respected"

provisions.text_summary: Immediate enactment clause tied to simple-majority passage and signature.
provisions.exact_change: Do not treat the enactment clause as current Code text.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be declared law once passed"

current_implementation.detail: The later-law decision for PL 29-210 records no present XML operation for PL 15-104. The law is now only repealed-history material.

recommended_actions: Leave PL 15-104 as repealed or superseded history only.
Do not create or preserve a live Code insertion for its military-justice framework.
Carry forward only the repeal cross-reference from PL 29-210.

laws.title: Public Law 15-105 | Municipal Prosecution Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is operative and continues to govern the District of Columbia prosecution structure. It contains express repeals and substitutions affecting earlier District-law provisions, but no later law in the reviewed materials repeals PL 15-105 itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the title and purpose as note material.
provisions.evidence: "remove the District Attorney General's Office for the District of Columbia"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Part D of PL 12-88.
provisions.exact_change: Mark Part D of PL 12-88 repealed.
provisions.evidence: "Part D shall be hereby repealed"

provisions.text_summary: Delegates DC municipal and federal prosecution to the U.S. Attorney's Office and allows special prosecutors.
provisions.exact_change: Record the DOJ/U.S. Attorney prosecution delegation.
provisions.evidence: "shall have the sole responsibility for prosecuting both municipal and federal laws"

provisions.text_summary: Revises the recall process and repeals Title 6 section 37 of PL 12-88.
provisions.exact_change: Mark the recall provision repealed and note the replacement process.
provisions.evidence: "Title 6, Section 37 ... shall be repealed"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals two PL 15-97 provisions and substitutes section 103(b)(v).
provisions.exact_change: Apply the strike-and-insert text to PL 15-97.
provisions.evidence: "Section 103, Sub-Section iii shall be repealed"

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the enactment clause as note material.
provisions.evidence: "This Resolution will take effect immediately"

current_implementation.detail: The live note treatment is too generic. The note should record the express repeals and substitutions, and the DC prosecution provisions fit title 28 better than a generic placeholder.

recommended_actions: Implement the repeal-marking for PL 12-88 Part D and Title 6 section 37.
Carry the PL 15-97 strike-and-insert text into the affected law.
Replace the boilerplate note with a concise title 28 statutory note.

laws.title: Public Law 15-106 | Congress Strikes Back Repealment
laws.status_basis: The act is a live repealing statute. Its substantive effect is to repeal The Congress Strikes Back Act, while the repealing statute itself remains current.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as note material.
provisions.evidence: "The Congress Strikes Back Repealment"

provisions.text_summary: Findings about the earlier act's constitutionality.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the reasoning as a short historical note.
provisions.evidence: "cannot co-exist with the United States Constitution"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals The Congress Strikes Back Act in full.
provisions.target: The Congress Strikes Back Act of 2022
provisions.exact_change: Mark The Congress Strikes Back Act as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "shall be repealed in its entirety"

current_implementation.detail: The current note misclassifies the repealing statute itself as repealed or superseded and leaves the actual repeal unimplemented. The act should stay current and be summarized as a repealing note.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to The Congress Strikes Back Act.
Replace the boilerplate with a concise note identifying PL 15-106 as the repealing statute.
Remove the archive/Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-107 | Guidance to Guidances Act
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable. No authenticated enactment text could be reconstructed, and the title appears to duplicate PL 15-101's recovered source text. The law should not be invented from the gap.

provisions.text_summary: Source unrecoverable; possible duplicate of PL 15-101, but not safely reconstructable.
provisions.exact_change: No operative text can be safely determined.
provisions.evidence: No readable authenticated enactment text could be recovered.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML uses boilerplate repeal-history language, but the source is actually unrecoverable. The note should say that the source is missing rather than pretending certainty.

recommended_actions: Retain only a source-limited historical note.
Flag the possible duplicate with PL 15-101 for review.
Remove the boilerplate status prose and archive link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-108 | Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The recovered source sets internal congressional document-labeling rules. It is substantive public-law text, but it is not positive-law Code text and should be treated as note material or excluded from permanent Code sections.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a concise congressional-procedure note.
provisions.evidence: "The Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023"

provisions.text_summary: Creates label conventions for bills, resolutions, amendments, committee reports, and treaty documents.
provisions.exact_change: Record the rules only as internal congressional procedure material.
provisions.evidence: "H.R.# and S.# shall be only used when bills are being proposed."
provisions.notes: This is not a Code rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: The note is anchored in title 50, which is the wrong subject matter for a congressional-labeling act. The entry should either move to title 2 or remain a concise historical note only.

recommended_actions: Re-anchor to title 2 if the repository wants a note entry at all.
Do not treat the labeling rules as positive-law Code amendments.
Remove the archive/Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-109 | Ending Tigray for the Last Time
laws.status_basis: The source is a current joint resolution whose substantive effect is to repeal House Joint Resolution 6. It is the repealing instrument, not the repealed object.

provisions.text_summary: Formally repeals House Joint Resolution 6 in full.
provisions.exact_change: Mark H.J. Res. 6 as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "shall be formally repealed in its entirety"
provisions.notes: War-powers wind-down; keep as a concise historical note.

current_implementation.detail: The existing note misstates PL 15-109 itself as repealed or superseded. The real action is the repeal of H.J. Res. 6, so the note should be historical and current-law-aware.

recommended_actions: Record the repeal of House Joint Resolution 6.
Keep PL 15-109 only as a concise historical note.
Remove the archive/Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 15-110 | A Bill to Direct the Inspector General of the Department of Defense to Conduct an Investigation Into the Attack on the United States Embassy in Damascus, and for Other Purposes
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable. No authenticated statutory text could be verified, and the title suggests a one-time investigative directive that would not normally warrant permanent Code text even if recovered.

provisions.text_summary: Unrecoverable DoD IG investigative directive.
provisions.exact_change: None determinable from the source record.
provisions.evidence: No readable authenticated enactment text could be recovered.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML uses boilerplate repealed-or-superseded language, but the source is actually unreadable. The note should disclose the source limitation instead of claiming certainty.

recommended_actions: Retain only a source-limited historical note.
Do not invent operational text for the investigative directive.
Remove the archive/Trello link and boilerplate status prose.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source creates a criminal prohibition on obstructing a presidential transition, defines the key terms, and sets a short jail-and-fine penalty. No later law in the reviewed materials repeals it.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as note material.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the Presidential Transition Protection Act"

provisions.text_summary: Defines presidential transition and President-elect.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the transition definitions as note material.
provisions.evidence: "Presidential transition shall be defined as"; "President-elect shall be defined as"

provisions.text_summary: Defines obstruction of a presidential transition and makes obstruction unlawful.
provisions.exact_change: Use title 18 for the offense note.
provisions.evidence: "Obstruction of a Presidential Transition"; "it will be prohibited"

provisions.text_summary: Sets a five-to-seven-day jail term and/or a $1,000 fine.
provisions.exact_change: Record the penalty note with the offense.
provisions.evidence: "five (5) days imprisonment" ... "one thousand dollars ($1,000)"

provisions.text_summary: Disclaims any future office disqualification from conviction.
provisions.exact_change: Record the no-office-bar clause as note material.
provisions.evidence: "shall be barred from holding public office"

current_implementation.detail: The current note sits in title 49, which is the wrong subject matter for a presidential-transition criminal-offense statute. The act belongs with title 3 for the transition context and title 18 for the offense.

recommended_actions: Re-anchor the offense material to title 18 and the transition context to title 3.
Replace the boilerplate with a concise statutory note.
Remove the archive/Trello link.
